key_points: Students actively subleasing to escape campus housing
Off-campus options start around $730-$950/month near TSU
Dorm quality described as outdated; alumni note little change since 2004
key_quotes: I get no concept of the size of the campus from this video. What do the other buildings look like? Neighborhood?
that tiger walk was the place to be, I heard they have really remodeled but the apartment looks the same 'class of 04'
rent is $730 without parking!!

key_points: Campus culture is heavily club- and fashion-focused, not study-focused
Out-of-state students report serious culture shock, especially from Northeast
Local girls reportedly hostile toward out-of-state women
key_quotes: I didn't want a fashion show I wanted to hang out in sweats and study. And that's not TSU lol.
I'm shocked to hear they're still big on clubbing.
I think the local girls also hate on a lot of the out of state girls.

key_points: One student left partly due to difficulty finding familiar food
Houston food scene is rich but students must know where to look
No specific on-campus dining complaints but also no praise
key_quotes: the fact you said you couldn't find good soul food girl where were you going, it's literally the SOUTH home of soul food
Obviously you wasn't hitting up the right spots or people was not telling you about the right spots.
'No more Golden Corral': Inside Texas Southern's nutrition-led turnaround

key_points: No dedicated mental health resources mentioned by any student
First-year adjustment struggles noted, especially for out-of-state students
Homesickness and culture shock cited as reasons students transfer out
key_quotes: The first year away from home is always an adjustment.
I went to TSU from the west coast without visiting as well and definitely experienced the same culture shock.
You'll change your mind in about 10 years.

key_points: Financial aid disbursement is chaotic and dangerously slow
Students threatened with class drops while waiting for aid packages
Scholarship money raised publicly does not visibly reach students in need
key_quotes: easy to get in. hard to get out. it's so hard to graduate and it has nothing to do with classes/professors
I called them and they told me they never received my application, and they were ready to hang up after that.

I'm a current TSU student, close to graduation, and I've hit every wall you can think of: No Pell eligibility left, told there are no summer scholarships, reached out to financial aid, advisors, provost departments, and still no [answers].

key_points: Graduation is described as genuinely difficult despite easy admission
Bureaucratic obstacles, not coursework difficulty, block degree completion
Strong programs exist but require student to be proactive and self-directed
key_quotes: easy to get in. hard to get out. it's so hard to graduate and it has nothing to do with classes/professors
good school, just depends on your goals. as long as you DO good, you'll be fine.
I want to apply for Graduate Program in Biological Science for Spring 2022. Please Give some instruction about this program.

key_points: Financial aid office loses paperwork, gives incorrect info, and hangs up on students
Students threatened with class drops days before semester starts
Graduation ticket caps reversed only after public student protest
key_quotes: I called them and they told me they never received my application, and they were ready to hang up after that.
Got a letter from accounting said they will drop my classes Friday if not paid. Anyone else experiencing this?
Wow. Thanks for the hundreds of thousands of dollars here's your 4 tickets. Greedy university don't care about the students they just want your money!!!

key_points: Located in Houston, minutes from University of Houston campus
Campus infrastructure is aging; power lines and outdated buildings noted
Central Houston location is a genuine asset for internships and city life
key_quotes: I wonder if there's anything we can do to get those power lines moved off campus takes up a lot of space
the central location is nice, there's history, a good offering of degree programs
TSU and UofH are not far from each other. Matter of fact, they're at walking distance from each other.

key_points: Alumni report solid outcomes when they stayed academically focused
Strong band and performance arts pipeline with national recognition
NFL scouts visiting campus suggests some athletics-to-pro pathway exists
key_quotes: I went to UH but know several who went to TSU, we all turned out fine.
NFL Scouts Watch Texas Southern Players Shine at Prairie View Pro Day
as long as you DO good, you'll be fine.

key_points: Estimated total cost around $32k/year including mandatory fees
Aid delays and poor disbursement make budgeting nearly impossible
Recommended as a fallback for students with low stats who need a start
key_quotes: I saw that schooling costs an estimated 32k.
I'm coming in fall and my plan is to stay off campus. I wanted my aid to go towards an apartment.
Definitely apply to TSU and see how it goes and if you get any money.

key_points: Strong HBCU pride and community identity, especially around band and dance
Social scene dominates; academics take a backseat culturally
Best fit for students from the South or Houston who understand the culture
key_quotes: I am a proud TSU tiger!! Having fun and serving the people!!!
Really wished I'd gone to UH across the street.
one of the main reasons for going away to college is to grow, become independent, and get your degrees.

key_points: **Financial Aid:** Aid not disbursed before semester start; classes threatened with drops
**Administration:** Staff loses applications, gives wrong info, and disconnects calls
**Graduation:** Transcript withheld over unpaid balances with no payment plan offered
key_quotes: I called them and they told me they never received my application, and they were ready to hang up after that.
they said they don't [do a] payment plan [for transcripts]
easy to get in. hard to get out. it's so hard to graduate and it has nothing to do with classes/professors

key_points: TSU Stingettes dance team is nationally respected with a passionate following
Band culture and HBCU traditions are genuinely world-class
Proximity to UH creates informal cross-campus academic and social opportunities
key_quotes: They are def the 'Motion' of the Ocean! Her confidence just spreads throughout the squad.
TSU wins Third Straight SWAC Indoor Track & Field Championship, Captures 13th Overall Title
TXSU and UofH are at walking distance from each other.
